title: Exallage fulva
intro: Exallage fulva, also known as Crossopteryx ballyi or Crossopteryx fulva, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The bioactive constituents of Exallage fulva include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional African medicine, Exallage fulva is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, malaria, and respiratory infections.
